ops.text:
## Lane: item 3 — stage-limit sizing (analysis only) — COMPLETE. Cap $0, $0 spent (no LLM runs).

Recommendation: config-change numbers, NOT applied. Constants live in cc_steer/pipeline.py (TRIAGE_LIMIT=400, REFINE_LIMIT=400, ENRICH_LIMIT=200, AUDIT_BUDGET=60). Inputs: this log's rig-lane per-row cost table + schedule read from cc_steer/cli.py pipeline_install_launchd + cc_steer/pipeline.py run_pipeline.

### Assumed window + cap (stated explicitly; operator must confirm the cap)
- SCHEDULE (measured from cli.py): nightly `cc-steer pipeline run --auto-weekly` fires 03:00 local (launchd default --hour 3). Sunday folds in audit+eval. The weekly retrain (Sunday 04:00) is `cc-steer retrain` = Tinker(hosted)+mlx(local) MODEL TRAINING — it does NOT draw the Claude subscription usage pool, so it does not shrink the Claude budget (only overlaps local CPU). Effective overnight Claude window ≈ 03:00→~08:00 = ~5 h, which is also one Claude rolling-5h usage window.
- CAP ASSUMPTION: Claude Max 20x; a conservative usable OVERNIGHT single-window Opus budget of ~15M Opus tokens/night (~$146 API-equiv), reserving the rest of the 03:00-anchored 5h window so a surge night can't lock out the 08:00 user. Sonnet is a separate, much larger pool → enrich is never the bottleneck. The exact subscription token cap is NOT measurable from this lane; the formula below lets the operator plug in their real cap.

### LLM-consuming stages + measured per-row (production tiers)
- triage (tier=large/opus): $0.372/row, 35.24K tok/row  [measured]
- refine (tier=large/opus): $0.172/row, 21.31K tok/row  [measured]
- enrich (tier=medium/sonnet): ~$0.07/row, ~35.9K tok/row  [ESTIMATED — proxied from sonnet-triage; enrich pair-context likely ≤ this; separate sonnet pool]
- audit (tier=large/opus, WEEKLY only): $0.372/row, 35.24K tok/row; AUDIT_BUDGET=60 ⇒ 60 accepts+60 rejects = 120 rows
- scan / negatives(200) / reactions / export / eval = MECHANICAL, no LLM.

### KEY FINDING — wall-clock is NOT the binding constraint; the Opus usage cap is.
Stages run SEQUENTIALLY. Ceiling-night LLM wall @ concurrency=8:
- optimistic (throughput scales linearly from measured conc): triage 400/0.358=18.6m + refine 400/0.432=15.4m + enrich 200/0.384=8.7m ≈ 43 min
- conservative (TPM-bound, no gain past measured conc): triage 400/0.224=29.8m + refine 400/0.162=41.2m + enrich 200/0.24=13.9m ≈ 85 min
Either way a full-ceiling night finishes in 43–85 min, deep inside the ~5h (300 min) window — wall-clock would permit ~3–5x the current limits. But the Opus token/usage budget binds long before that:
- Current ceiling nightly (Mon–Sat): triage 400 + refine 400 + enrich 200
  opus = 400×35.24K + 400×21.31K = 14.10M + 8.53M = 22.63M tok  (~$231.6 API-equiv; sonnet enrich = 7.17M / $14)
- Sunday ceiling: + audit 120×35.24K = +4.23M opus ⇒ 26.86M opus / $276.2.
22.6M opus/night is large vs a single Max-20x overnight rolling-window Opus allowance → a backlog-surge night at the ceiling can exhaust the overnight window and, with 429 backoff, spill past 08:00 into the user's workday window.

### Sizing formula (operator plugs in real cap)
nightly opus tokens = 35.24K·TRIAGE + 21.31K·REFINE ≤ B_opus ; enrich sonnet = 35.9K·ENRICH ≤ B_sonnet(roomy) ; Sunday adds 35.24K×(2·AUDIT).
Keep REFINE ≲ ⅔·TRIAGE (refine draw is gated by triage accept-for-refinement rate < 1).
| B_opus/night | TRIAGE/REFINE | opus tok | API-equiv $ |
|---|---|---|---|
| 10M tight | 200 / 150 | 10.25M | $100 |
| 15M DEFAULT (Max20x conservative) | 300 / 200 | 14.83M | $146 |
| 22M current | 400 / 400 | 22.63M | $232 |
| 30M loose/dedicated acct | 500 / 500 | 28.28M | $290 |

### RECOMMENDED new limits (under the stated 15M-opus/night assumption)
- TRIAGE_LIMIT: 400 → 300   (300×35.24K = 10.57M opus, $111.6)
- REFINE_LIMIT: 400 → 200   (200×21.31K = 4.26M opus, $34.4)  ⇒ nightly opus 14.83M (~$146) ✓ under 15M
- ENRICH_LIMIT: 200 → 250   (250×35.9K = 8.97M SONNET, $17.5; optional bump — sonnet pool is cheap+separate, gated by refine output; keeping 200 is also fine)
- AUDIT_BUDGET: keep 60; drop to 40 (⇒80 rows, 2.82M opus) ONLY if the Sunday opus stack is a concern — Sunday@default = 14.83M+4.23M = 19.06M opus, the peak-risk night.
Net: still drains realistic surges (typical nightly new-row draw is tens of rows << ceiling; a 5776-row re-judge surge drains ~19 nights at 300 vs ~14 at 400 — safety-vs-catchup tradeoff made explicit). If the operator confirms a LOOSER cap / dedicated pipeline account, keep 400/400 (or raise per the table); wall-clock supports it.

### Usage-exhaustion risk flags
1. Sunday stacking: triage+refine+audit all opus in one window (19M @ default) — the night most likely to exhaust the overnight window.
2. 429-backoff spillover: a surge night's tail past ~08:00 lands the accumulated usage in the 03:00-anchored window right as the user starts work (window resets only at 08:00).
3. Enrich cost/tokens are ESTIMATED, not measured.
4. Measured $ is v6 (static-in-user → full per-row cache re-creation, cache_read=0). If item-2 (static→system cache) lands, triage is ~32% cheaper ⇒ the same B_opus supports ~440 triage rows — re-baseline limits after adoption.
5. Dominant lever is OPUS (triage+refine+audit ≈ 97% of nightly opus $); sonnet/enrich is near-free. Adjacent (out of sizing scope): moving refine or triage to sonnet ~5x's the row budget for the same usage.

### Monitor after adoption
- Per-pass opus token total (cache_creation+cache_read+input+output across triage/refine/audit envelopes) vs B_opus; alert on ceiling-hit (judged==limit = surge).
- `N pending` in the pass summary_line per stage — persistently rising ⇒ ceiling below incoming rate (catch-up losing).
- 429/transient markers + per-stage wall (rig's transient_markers/retries_est); wall spilling past ~08:00 or 429 spike ⇒ overnight window exhausting → lower limit or concurrency.
- Weekly cumulative opus usage vs the plan's weekly cap.

Artifacts: cc_steer/pipeline.py (constants), cc_steer/cli.py pipeline_install_launchd (schedule), ~/.cc-steer/experiments/WFA/results/{cal-triage,cal-triage-sonnet,cal-refine-opus}.json (per-row cost). $0 spent, no LLM invoked.
